Personal collection / panel study

Six-panel vertical slice

Manga Panel Gallery

A curated sequence of favorite manga panels, paced like a small exhibition where paper, ink, and notation stay quiet enough for the artwork to lead.

6 panelsCanonical local records

Selected sequence

Six panels arranged as a reading room.

The sequence keeps each image intact and lets the rhythm come from scale, proportion, and marginal notes rather than identical cards.

  1. 01

    Straw Hat at Dawn

    One Piece / Eiichiro Oda / Chapter 1 / Romance Dawn / East Blue / 1997 / Portrait

    A quiet promise becomes the emotional scale for an entire voyage, turning a small gesture into a lifelong horizon.

    Open panel
  2. 02

    Swordsman in the Rain

    Vagabond / Takehiko Inoue / Chapter 117 / Kyoto / 2001 / Landscape

    The horizontal pause lets weather and silence carry the scene, making restraint feel heavier than action.

    Open panel
  3. 03

    Alchemy Circle

    Fullmetal Alchemist / Hiromu Arakawa / Chapter 23 / Central City / 2003 / Square

    The square composition compresses grief and calculation into a single emblem, precise enough to feel ceremonial.

    Open panel
  4. 04

    Wall of Titans

    Attack on Titan / Hajime Isayama / Chapter 50 / Clash of the Titans / 2013 / Double-page spread

    The spread uses impossible scale as the subject, forcing the reader to measure human fear against the page itself.

    Open panel
  5. 05

    Karasuno Final Jump

    Haikyu!! / Haruichi Furudate / Chapter 292 / Nationals / 2018 / Portrait

    The panel turns athletic timing into bright vertical lift, letting bodies and white space share the same rush upward.

    Open panel
  6. 06

    Moonlit Train Platform

    Demon Slayer / Koyoharu Gotouge / Chapter 66 / Mugen Train / 2017 / Landscape

    The low, quiet angle gives the aftermath room to breathe, making courage register through stillness instead of spectacle.

    Open panel
